rationale: In the conceptual framework, the citing paper uses this reference as part of the theoretical basis for an exclusivity mechanism, not merely as broad background. Specifically, it cites conspicuous-consumption research to support the claim that exclusivity/status benefits can increase willingness to pay, which is then mapped to the app's paid-vs-free user setting. The paper does not reuse the cited paper's formal model, but it does rely on its core idea to motivate hypotheses about the interaction between soft landing and exclusive secondary offerings.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the cited paper's exclusivity/status-value insight to a new context: monetization of a previously free app. It uses that idea to argue that exclusive secondary offerings can create extra value for paying users when free users remain on the platform.
